assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan and file descriptions reference inspecting multiple sibling files. file-1-description.md lists 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs — AdvisorySummary struct' and 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/details.rs — AdvisoryDetails struct' as Sibling References. file-2-description.md lists 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs — GET handler for /api/v2/advisory/{id}' as a Sibling Reference. conventions.md explicitly references 'advisory/endpoints/get.rs' for handler patterns and 'common/src/error.rs' for error handling. file-4-description.md references 'Existing methods in this file: fetch, list, search' for advisory/service/advisory.rs. At least 4 of the specified files are referenced: get.rs, advisory.rs (service), summary.rs, and common/src/error.rs.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files Overview' lists exactly 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs test) and 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs). The plan explicitly confirms 'server/src/main.rs' under 'Files Confirmed Unchanged' and the git add command in 'Git Commands' section lists only these 6 files. No unrelated files appear in any of the file descriptions (files 1-6).

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Create' table lists: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' — SeveritySummary response struct, (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' — GET handler, (3) 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' — Integration tests. Corresponding file descriptions file-1-description.md, file-2-description.md, and file-3-description.md all have 'Action: CREATE' and provide detailed implementation content for each.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Modify' table lists: (4) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' — Add severity_summary method, (5) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' — Register route, (6) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' — Add pub mod severity_summary. Corresponding file descriptions file-4-description.md, file-5-description.md, and file-6-description.md all have 'Action: MODIFY' and provide detailed change descriptions.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Commit Message' section shows: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' as the subject line (type=feat, scope=advisory, description follows colon), with 'Implements TC-9201' in the footer. The full commit command in 'Git Commands' repeats this same message. The format matches Conventional Commits: type(scope): description.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Git Commands' section includes: "git commit --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' -m ..." on line 139. Additionally, conventions.md line 98 states: 'Must include --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"'.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 8 states 'Branch: TC-9201'. The 'Git Commands' section on line 128 shows 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. conventions.md line 101-102 states: 'Feature branches named after Jira issue ID (e.g., TC-9201)'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md identifies both patterns explicitly: (1) Error handling — line 47-49: 'The AppError enum in common/src/error.rs implements Axum's IntoResponse. Errors are wrapped with .context("descriptive message") before returning.' and line 37: 'All service methods return Result<T, AppError> using .context() wrapping from common/src/error.rs.' (2) Module structure — lines 16-18: 'Every domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ three-directory pattern. Each directory has a mod.rs that re-exports public items and registers sub-modules. Siblings: sbom/, advisory/, package/ all follow this exact layout.'
